title: Citrusy Dutch Blackeye Pea Hash
description: A quick-fix Dutch breakfast dish featuring blackeye peas, kombu, and citrus flavors.
instructions: Soak the blackeye peas in water for at least 8 hours or overnight.
Drain and rinse the blackeye peas and set aside.
In a large pot, combine the kombu and 4 cups of water. Bring to a boil and then reduce heat to a simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the kombu and add the drained blackeye peas, onion, and garlic to the pot. Simmer for 20 minutes or until the peas are tender.
In a separate pan, heat the coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the lemon and orange juice to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the blackeye pea mixture to the pan and stir to combine.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the hash hot, garnished with additional citrus juice if desired.
